entities.name: TANZANIA
entities.description: A country in East Africa where community seed production projects were studied

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: The continent where community seed production projects are being implemented

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop produced and sold by small-scale farmers in the studied projects

entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop produced and sold by small-scale farmers in the studied projects

entities.name: COMMUNITY SEED PRODUCTION PROJECTS
entities.description: Programs designed to encourage small-scale farmers to produce and sell seed varieties

entities.name: SEED MULTIPLICATION
entities.description: The process of increasing the quantity of seed, identified as a common problem to be solved

entities.name: SEED DISTRIBUTION
entities.description: The process of delivering seed to farmers, identified as a common problem to be solved

entities.name: SEED QUALITY CONTROL
entities.description: Training provided to growers to ensure the quality of produced seeds

entities.name: CERTIFIED SEED
entities.description: A specific category of seed with a certified quality standard

entities.name: QUALITY DECLARED SEED
entities.description: A category of seed where quality is declared by the producer

entities.name: COMMON GRADE SEED
entities.description: A basic category of seed without formal certification or declaration

entities.name: SOURCE SEED
entities.description: The initial seed used for multiplication, requiring external investment for production and delivery

entities.name: SEED MARKETING
entities.description: The process of selling seeds, identified as the biggest threat to program viability

entities.name: PUBLIC INVESTMENT
entities.description: Funding from the government sector necessary for the long-term sustainability of seed projects

entities.name: PRIVATE SECTOR INVESTMENTS
entities.description: Funding from private entities involved in seed production and distribution
